folio.label: 54v
folio.l: For swicú lawe as a man ©euetú , a noæä wygút
He shulde hi~ selue vsen it , be rigút
Thus wyl oure text , but natheles certeyn
I can rigút now , no thrifty tale seyn
But Chaucer , thougú he can but lewedly
On meetris , and on rymyngö craftily
Hatú seid hem , in swicú englissú as he can
Of olde tyme , as knowetú many a man
And if he haue nou©t seid he~ , leue brother
In o boke , he hath seid he~ , in a nother
For he hath told of louyers , vp and downe
Moo than Ouyde made of , mencione
In hise epistles , that ben ful olde
What shulde I telle he~ , syn they ben tolde
In ©outhe he made , of Ceys and Alcione
And sithen hatú he spoken , of euäychone
Theise noble wyues , and theise louyers eke
Who so æat wyl , his large volume seke
Cleped the Seyntes legende , of cupide
There may he sen , the large woundes wyde
Of lucresse , and of babilan Tesbe
The swerd of Dido , for the fals Eue
The tree of phillis , for hir demophon
The pleynte of Diane , and of hermyou~
Of Adriane , and Isiphilee
The baraigne Isle , stondynge in the see
The dreynte leandre , for his erro
The teeres of Eleyne , and eke the woo
Of Brixseide , and of the ladomya
The cruelte , of the Quene Medea

note.notetxt: 72after this line the scribe changed pen and used a new one or remade the point. It is clear that this pen do not leave heavy stroke of ink on the paper.ODR

folio.l: Thy litel children hangynge , by the hals
For thy Ioson that was of loue so fals
O îmystre , Penolape Alceste
ñoure wyfhode , ©e comende with the beste
But certeynly , no word wrytetú he
Of thilke wykked ensaumple , of Canacee
That loued hire owen brother , synfully
Of swiche cursed stories , I seye fy
Or ellis of Tiro Appollonius
How æ the cursed kyngö , Antiochus
Biraft hire dougúter , of hire maydenhede
That is so horrible a tale , for to rede
Whan he hire threwe , vp on the paument

note.notetxt: 85These lines are crammed. 86r the first three lines were already written. It is obvious even if the ink is the sameODR
